creator: fernape@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to alt2600 from comment #4)
Done in a8d3668988945bf23844a1733da214113e08d6e

> perhaps I read too much into your initial response, but some folks do like to 
> point to "it works in poudriere" and seemingly not care it doesn't work in ports, 
> and I was feeling extra sensitive with some windows server upgrade issues I've 
> been dealing with at work with a database cluster I service. At least it appears 
> this won't require adding to my custom patch sets to get locally produced 
> documentation for offline reference. Thanks

No worries. Ports are still here, but for a reproducible (so to speak) build with guarantees, a clean environment is desirable or even required due to incompatibilities in dependencies, options, etc. Poudriere provides that and things like package seeding which downloads dependencies from the official repositories it available, saving a lot of time :-)

Cheers.
